behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parser/dependency graph resolver -> vulnerability-check input builder. A unit test of only one parser function can miss dropped transitive deps during wiring. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in this behavior text; no AI/ML output; no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is extraction/inclusion logic, not real HTTP/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + transitive resolver + check-request builder together on fixture manifests/lockfiles; assert the emitted vulnerability-check payload contains every expected direct and transitive package@version.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is typically at query-construction + response-filtering boundary across scanner modules. Minimum infra is a deterministic fake vuln DB contract endpoint/data; no need for real internet behavior to catch version-range mismatch bugs. Integration-brake: not OS/network-condition dependent by wording, no AI/ML, no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text focuses on semantic matching of version ranges, not transport-level failures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire query builder + DB client adapter + result matcher against a local stub dataset containing same package with multiple vulnerable ranges; assert only CVEs matching manifest version constraints are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is pure ordering logic on provided records (single function/object boundary, no I/O). Mock-miss check: failure is in local comparator/ordering algorithm, not module interaction. Rejection-gate: Integration/System rejected because behavior text is explicit sort rule only, with no cross-module or real infra dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorting function with mixed severities and equal-severity CVEs with different CVSS values; assert exact output order.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when parser -> updater -> serializer modules interact (e.g., accidental mutation of unrelated entries). A unit test on only the updater function can miss serialization/wiring regressions. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in wording; no AI/ML; no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is content-preservation in manifest edit, not OS file-event or external service behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end manifest rewrite pipeline in-process on fixture files/objects; assert only target dependency version changed and all other keys/versions remain byte- or structurally-equivalent per format expectations.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository/PR creation boundary. Mocks can hide auth, branch, git, and provider API integration failures. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both trigger System: real VCS + real HTTP API interaction required to catch true failure.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, run scanner remediation path and verify an actual PR is opened; assert PR diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated file changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Single deterministic semver decision rule; no I/O, no module orchestration required to expose core failure. Rejection-gate: Integration/System rejected because behavior text is direct semver comparison logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver classifier function with version pairs (major/minor/patch/pre-release cases); assert only major increments are marked breaking.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff: semver detector -> remediation decision -> PR creator gate -> report emitter. Need modules wired to catch regressions where skip/report diverge. Integration-brake: behavior does not require real OS/network if PR client is a spy/fake; no AI/ML output; no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is decision-and-output gating, not real repository creation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector, remediation orchestrator, PR service interface (fake/spy), and report formatter; feed a breaking bump and assert PR service is not called while output contains review-required flag.
